folio.label: 65r
folio.l: Thus can oure lady , bryngen out of woo
Wooful Custaunce , and many a nother moo
And longe tyme dwelled she/ in that place
In holy werkes euäe , as was hire grace
¢ The Senatours wyf , hire Aunte was
But for al that , she knew hire neuäe the moore
I wyl no lengere tary , in this caas
But to the kyngö Alla , whicú I spak of ©ore
That wepeth for his wyf , and sygúetú sore
I wyl returne , and lete I wyl Custaunce
Vnder the Senatours , gouernaunce
¢ Kyngö Alla , whicú , æat hatú , his moder slayn
Vp on a day fel , in swicú repentaunce
That if I shortly tellen shal , and pleyn
To Rome he cometú , to resceyue his penaunce
And putte hi~ , in the Popes ordinaunce
In heigú and low , and iúu crist bysougút
For©eue hise wykked werkes , that he wrougút
¢ The fame a noon , thurgú out the toun is born~
How Alla kyngö , shal come on pilgrymage
By herbegeours , that wenten hi~ byforn~
For whicú the Senatour , as it was vsage
Rood hi~ a ©ein , and many of his lynage
As wel to shewen , his heigú magnificence
As to doon , any kyngö , a reuerence
¢ Gret cher maketú , this noble Senatour
To kyngö Alla , and he to hym also
Euäyche of hem , dotú other greet honour
And so byfeÂ , that with inne a day or twoo
This Senatour is , to kyngö Alla goo
To feeste , and shortly , if I shal nat lye
Custaunces sone , went in his companye
¢ So~me men wold seyn , that at the request of Custaunce
This Senatour hatú lad , this child to feste
I may nat tellen , euäy circumstaunce
Be as be may , there was he at the leste
But sotú is , that , at his modres heeste
Byforn~ Alla , duryngö the metes space
The child stood , lokyngö in the kynges face
¢ This Alla kyngö , hatú of the child greet wonder
And to the Senatour , he seide a noon
Whoos is this fair child , that stondetú ©onder
I not q¶ he , by god , and by Seynt Ioún
